title: Annickia affinis
intro: Annickia affinis, also known as the African bush mango, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Annonaceae. The health benefits of Annickia affinis include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been found to exhibit cytotoxic and antimalarial activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Annickia affinis are traditional medicines, such as decoctions and infusions, which are used in African folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Annickia affinis is classified as a species of plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. It belongs to the genus Annickia, a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. The family Annonaceae is a large and diverse group of plants, consisting of over 2,000 species. Annickia affinis is a climbing plant that is native to West and Central Africa, where it is often found in tropical forests and woodlands. The classification of Annickia affinis reflects its close relationship with other plants in the Annonaceae family.
